reasoning: The most advanced Shigellosis vaccine candidate (S4V2, LimmaTech/Valneva) is only in Phase 2/2b trials as of H1 2025, with results expected in the second half of the year. There are currently no approved vaccines for Shigellosis globally. Given that the leading candidate will not even finish Phase 2 studies until late 2025, it cannot be approved or rolled out in 2025. Thus, it is extremely unlikely that Wikipedia editors will update the "Vaccine(s)" status on the "List of infectious diseases" page to "Yes" securely by any of the 2025 resolution dates. I estimate the probability is effectively zero, but bound my answers at the 0.05 minimum dictated by the rules, slightly increasing to 0.06 for November to account for any premature edits by Wikipedia editors following Phase 2b data releases.
